questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She went to the gym ___"
questions.answer: to get fit. (purpose)

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "My doctor advised me ___"
questions.answer: to eat less sugar. (verb + object + infinitive)
to change jobs next year. (verb + infinitive)
to learn a language as an adult. (adjective + infinitive)

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She persuaded her friend ___"
questions.answer: to come to the party with her. (verb + object + infinitive)

questions.prompt: I went to the supermarket _____ (buy) some food for dinner.
questions.answer: to buy — Purpose: use the infinitive with 'to' to say why you did something.

questions.prompt: My teacher encouraged me _____ (apply) for the language course.
questions.answer: to apply — Verb + object + infinitive: 'encouraged me to apply' (encourage + person + to + verb).

questions.prompt: She agreed _____ (help) us move to our new flat.
questions.answer: to help — Verb + infinitive: 'agree' is directly followed by 'to + verb'.

questions.prompt: It is impossible _____ (please) everyone all the time.
questions.answer: to please — Adjective + infinitive: 'impossible to please' (adjective + to + verb).

questions.prompt: He warned us _____ (not touch) the hot surface.
questions.answer: not to touch — Verb + object + negative infinitive: 'warned us not to touch' (warn + person + not to + verb).

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She went to the shop for buy some milk."
questions.answer: Use the infinitive with 'to' for purpose, not 'for + verb': 'She went to the shop to buy some milk.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "My parents told me be home by ten."
questions.answer: Verb + object + infinitive needs 'to': 'My parents told me to be home by ten.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "He decided leaving his job after working there for ten years."
questions.answer: 'Decide' is followed by the infinitive, not the gerund: 'He decided to leave his job.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "It is very hard understand this grammar rule."
questions.answer: Adjective + infinitive needs 'to': 'It is very hard to understand this grammar rule.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She persuaded her sister apply for the scholarship."
questions.answer: Verb + object + infinitive needs 'to': 'She persuaded her sister to apply for the scholarship.'
She called me to ask for help.
My boss told me to arrive early.
She decided to travel.
It is hard to communicate.
They persuaded him to stay.
